session.study: Flight Centre AU Domestic Booking Flow - Usability Test

events.description: The screen transitions to a dark background, preparing to display task instructions.
Task instructions begin to appear on the screen, starting with 'Perfect! Here's your task...'
The task instructions continue to populate, detailing the scenario of booking a domestic flight in Australia.
Additional instructions are displayed, asking the user to think out loud during the process.
The final part of the task instructions is revealed on the screen.
The task instructions disappear, and the browser's bookmarks bar becomes visible again.
Scroll E (magnitude: 3.5, uniformity: 0.72)
User dwells on the Flight Centre homepage, likely reviewing the 'UK & EUROPE SALE' hero banner content as the cursor remains stationary.
events.frame_description: The Flight Centre homepage is displayed with its characteristic red header and logo; the browser address bar shows the flightcentre.com.au URL.

events.description: User selects February 18th as the departure date in the calendar, causing the modal header to change to 'Returning travel dates'
events.frame_description: The 'Travel dates' calendar modal is open. The user clicks on the 18th of February.

events.description: User selects February 21st as the return date in the calendar
events.frame_description: The 'Returning travel dates' calendar modal is open. The user clicks on the 21st of February.

events.description: User hovered over the '1 traveller' selection dropdown in the search widget.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the '1 traveller' dropdown menu within the flight search interface.

events.description: The screen is blank white, indicating the flight results page is in the process of loading.
events.frame_description: Blank white loading screen.

events.description: Scroll E (magnitude: 9.4, uniformity: 0.68)
User dwells on the flight results page, with the cursor resting near the bottom of the screen while viewing flight options from Melbourne (MEL) to Sydney (SYD) for Saturday 21 Feb.
events.frame_description: Flight search results header visible showing MEL to SYD for Sat 21 Feb.

events.description: User clicked on the 6:00 AM flight result to expand it and view available fare types.
events.frame_description: The view changes from the flight search results list to the specific fare selection screen for the 6:00 AM Qantas flight from Sydney to Melbourne.

events.description: User dwells on the 'Red Edeal' fare type details, likely reading the baggage and cancellation rules in the selection overlay.
events.frame_description: The 'Select Departing Fare Type' overlay is visible, showing details for the 'Red Edeal' fare.

events.description: The UI transitions to the return flight selection screen, displaying the message 'Good choice! It's time to choose your flight for your return journey...'
events.frame_description: Transition screen header showing flight route MEL to SYD and 'Select Departing' text.

events.description: User dwells on the transition screen while the return flight options are being prepared for display.
events.frame_description: Static view of the transition screen header during the loading process.

events.description: User examines the 'Choice' fare details for a Virgin Australia flight, including baggage and change fees
events.frame_description: Close-up of Virgin Australia Choice fare inclusions

events.description: User hovers the cursor over the 'Fare Rules' link for the 'Lite' fare option
events.frame_description: Cursor positioned over the Fare Rules link in the Lite fare column

events.description: The page transitions to the return flight selection screen after an outbound flight is selected
events.frame_description: Faded view of the 'Select Return Flight' header during a page transition

events.description: User reviews the selected outbound flight summary (7:35am MEL to SYD) at the top of the return flight selection page
events.frame_description: Summary card showing the selected outbound flight details

events.description: User scrolls down the 'Review trip' page to view the returning flight details.
events.frame_description: The view shifts from the departing flight details to the returning flight details.

events.description: User hovers over the 'ECONOMY' ticket class information in the flight summary section.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the red header area indicating the ticket class is Economy.

events.description: User scrolls down the page from the flight summary to the baggage options section.
events.frame_description: The view shifts from the top header area to a section detailing baggage tracking services.

events.description: User hovers over the baggage tracking service information, specifically near the 'See full' terms link.
events.frame_description: The cursor is resting on text describing a baggage tracking service for $14.95 per passenger.

events.description: User hovers over the 'NEXT FLIGHT' button in the seat selection modal.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the blue 'NEXT FLIGHT' button at the bottom right of the seat selection interface.

events.description: User hovers over the seat map area while reviewing options.
User clicks to select a seat, causing a loading spinner to appear.
The seat selection interface loads for Flight 2, displaying seat 5E as the selected option with pricing details.
events.frame_description: Loading spinner followed by the appearance of the seat map and a selection tooltip. seat 5E is highlighted. pricing for 'Preferred Seat' is shown. tab for 'Flight 2' is active. Qantas and Virgin Australia logos are visible. page title is 'Select Seats'. page location is 'secure.flightcentre.com.au/AAXy9ikH/results#baggage'. page title is 'Flight Centre Australia'. interaction target is 'Seat 5E'. confidence is 1.0. frame description is 'Loading spinner followed by the appearance of the seat map and a selection tooltip.'.

events.description: User clicks the 'CONFIRM' button to finalize the selection of seat 5E.
User scrolls down the page from the Lost Bag Guarantee section to the Priority Assistance section.
events.frame_description: The view shifts from flight extras like baggage and seats to priority assistance and price protection options.

events.description: User hovers the cursor over the 'Pay' step in the progress navigation bar
events.frame_description: The user is on the Travellers page (Step 2) and the cursor is positioned over the 'Pay' label in the red header progress bar.

events.description: The passenger details form finishes loading, replacing the skeleton screen placeholders.
events.frame_description: The UI transitions from placeholder skeleton elements to the actual 'Adult 1' passenger details form including name fields and a booking contact checkbox.

events.description: User scrolls down the 'Adult 1' passenger details form to reveal the Date of Birth and optional membership fields.
events.frame_description: The view shifts from the top of the passenger details form to the middle section containing rewards and frequent flyer options.

events.description: User pauses to examine the 'Price summary' section on the right side of the page.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned over the price summary sidebar while the user reviews the costs.

events.description: User scrolls further down to the bottom of the form, revealing the phone number, email fields, and marketing preference checkbox.
events.frame_description: The page content moves up to show the contact information and email subscription section.

events.description: User pauses while reviewing the contact information section, specifically near the email input field.
events.frame_description: The cursor rests in the main form area near the email field after scrolling.

events.description: User hovers the cursor over the 'Feedback' tab on the right side of the browser window.
events.frame_description: The cursor is positioned directly over the red vertical 'Feedback' button on the right edge of the screen.
